Question: A population of rabbits in a meadow is observed to be $$200$$ rabbits at time $$t = 0 .$$ After a month, the rabbit population is observed to have increased by $$4 \text{%} .$$ Using an initial population of $$200$$ and a growth rate of $$0.04 ,$$ with a carrying capacity of $$750$$ rabbits, Write the logistic differential equation and initial condition for this model. Draw a slope field for this logistic differential equation, and sketch the solution corresponding to an initial population of $$200$$ rabbits. Solve the initial-value problem for $$P ( t ) .$$ Use the solution to predict the population after $$1$$ year.
Answer: $$\frac{d P}{d t} = 0.04 ( 1 - \frac{P}{750} ) , P ( 0 ) = 200$$ $$P ( t ) = \frac{3000 e^{.04 t}}{11 + 4 e^{.04 t}}$$ After $$12$$ months, the population will be $$P ( 12 ) \approx 278$$ rabbits.
